title: Dead Lettering, Poison Messages, and Parking · Execution Trace: Dead Letter Trigger Route
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must interpret x-death evidence defensively, prevent cycles, retain bounded context, and authorize replay through a separate path; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dead letter trigger route increment in a Go poison-message remediation workflow. Learners pr…
